column1: # that compose to existing primitives. End-to-end verification that
# @ERR — parser emits the Excel `#VALUE!` literal. IronCalc 0.7.1's
# CellValue has no Error variant (it returns `String("#VALUE!")`),
# and the engine adapter currently passes that through verbatim.
# That display authenticity gap is tracked separately; here we
# assert only that the formula round-trips on the panel.
# Years to double an investment at 5%: LN(2)/LN(1.05) ≈ 14.2067.
# Seed three pairs in B1..B3 / C1..C3 for SUMPRODUCT.
# @CODE → UNICODE: codepoint of the first char of "A" is 65.
# @REPLACE(s, start, n, new) — IronCalc 0.7.1 has SUBSTITUTE but no
# @S(range) — IronCalc 0.7.1 has no @S equivalent. Emulated as
# Seed D1 with a label, D2 with a number; @S on each tells them apart.
# (B1..B3 are already seeded with 1,2,3 by the SUMPRODUCT test above.)
# @S(D2) returns "" (D2 is a number — empty label).
# @S on a multi-cell range returns the top-left cell's text.
# @CELLPOINTER("row") — should reflect the cell holding the formula.
# Place the call at A12; @CELLPOINTER("row") rewrites to @CELL("row",A12)
